headline: The Atlantic created a searchable database of the music used to train AI
summary: The Atlantic reporter Alex Reisner built a searchable database of music used to train AI models. It contains four datasets, two of which are massive with 12 million and 9 million tracks. This resource makes AI training data publicly accessible and transparent.

headline: Musician and YouTuber Hainbach on ‘Breath of the Wild’ and Swiss Army Knives
summary: Stefan Paul Goetsch, known as Hainbach, is an experimental composer and YouTuber. He creates music using unique methods, often with laboratory and scientific equipment. Hainbach likens his "hard mode" production techniques to the "Dark Souls of synthesis."

headline: Moves of the Diamond Hand is an unfinished, irresistibly weird dice-based RPG
summary: "Moves of the Diamond Hand" is a creative, dice-based roleplaying game. It features numerous strange conversations and constant dice rolling. Despite being unfinished, it offers a unique and engaging RPG experience.

headline: Toy Story has the right take on tech
summary: This article discusses "Toy Story's" perspective on technology. It's part of the "Installer" newsletter, focusing on "Verge-iest stuff." The author reflects on various tech-related topics through the lens of the beloved animated film.

headline: Epic Games Store Roadmap Promises 5x Faster Boot Times, User Reviews, and More
summary: The Epic Games Store is set to receive significant updates based on a new roadmap. Planned features include 5x faster boot times and the addition of user reviews. A major launcher redesign is also expected, addressing long-requested community features.

headline: The GTPLAYER Big and Tall Gaming Chair Drops to Less Than $160 Ahead of Amazon Prime Day
summary: The GTPLAYER Big and Tall Gaming Chair is on sale for under $160. This deal is available ahead of Amazon Prime Day. It's highlighted as a great gift, even with potentially belated delivery.

headline: Bambu 3D Printers Are on Sale at Best Buy Ahead of Amazon Prime Day
summary: Bambu 3D Printers are currently on sale at Best Buy. The discount comes ahead of Amazon Prime Day. It's presented as an opportune moment for 3D printing enthusiasts to upgrade.

headline: Save 50% Off a 12-Month Walmart+ Membership for Prime Day and Stream the 2026 FIFA World Cup
summary: Walmart+ memberships are 50% off for 12 months as a Prime Day deal. Benefits include early access to deals, free shipping, and free returns. Members also get Paramount+ streaming, allowing access to the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

headline: Iran says it has closed Strait of Hormuz over Israeli attacks in Lebanon
summary: Iran claims to have closed the strategic Strait of Hormuz. This action is stated as a response to Israeli attacks in Lebanon. The US military has disputed Iran's claim, ahead of US-Iran talks.

headline: Nine people in critical condition among 100 injured in fatal Bedford train crash
summary: A fatal train crash in Bedford resulted in one death and 100 injuries. Nine individuals are currently in critical condition. British Transport Police are investigating the cause of the collision.

headline: Talk of Starmer staying on to fight is fading - fast
summary: Support for Prime Minister Starmer is rapidly declining. This follows Andy Burnham's recent by-election victory. Laura Kuenssberg reports on the evaporating support for the leader.

headline: King Charles to reveal personal tax bill for first time as monarch
summary: King Charles will disclose his personal tax bill publicly for the first time. This disclosure will be part of the annual royal financial report. It marks a new level of transparency regarding the monarch's finances.
